mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decrease in the federal funds rate lowers the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, which increases equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns following month-over-month declines in FEDFUNDS do not exceed the unconditional mean return with a one-sided t-test p-value below 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points over a three-month window signals deteriorating household income expectations and triggers institutional de-risking, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances where UNRATE has risen more than 0.3 percentage points over the prior 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and industrial production indicates a coordinated demand-supply expansion that reduces recession probability, and this joint signal drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days beyond the effect of either series alone.
failure_condition: In months where both RSAFS and INDPRO increase, the interaction term fails to add incremental explanatory power for 20-day S&P 500 returns (F-test p > 0.10) and the subset does not yield significantly positive mean returns (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 3.0%, a month-over-month increase in the consumer price index signals persistent inflation that forces market participants to reprice terminal rate expectations higher, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.0%, S&P 500 5-day forward returns following month-over-month increases in CPIAUCSL are positive in more than 55% of observations, or the conditional Sharpe is not lower than the unconditional Sharpe.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the industrial production index of more than 1.0 point signals contracting manufacturing output, reduces corporate earnings forecasts, and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances where INDPRO declines by more than 1.0 index point month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Joint month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls and a decline in the unemployment rate signal accelerating labor market tightness that raises wage-push inflation concerns, prompting monetary policy repricing and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term of PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline does not significantly predict negative 60-day S&P 500 returns (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) and the effect size is not more negative than either single-series model.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 2.5%, a month-over-month increase in housing starts signals demand-led economic expansion that supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 2.5%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns following month-over-month increases in HOUST are not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) and do not exceed returns generated when FEDFUNDS is above 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales signals stronger consumer spending that lifts near-term corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns following month-over-month growth in RSAFS are not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) and the hit rate of positive returns is below 55%.
